useCasesPage.title: Use Cases
useCasesPage.description: Real-world applications for multi-chain private payments
useCasesPage.casinoDesc: Online casinos and gaming platforms can accept private crypto deposits and process ZK-private withdrawals while maintaining regulatory compliance. Players enjoy financial privacy while operators get comprehensive B2B infrastructure including merchant dashboard, dispute resolution, and automated liquidity management across Solana, Arbitrum, and TRON networks.
useCasesPage.casinoStep1: Casino integrates ZKIRA Gateway API with merchant dashboard access
useCasesPage.casinoStep2: Player initiates deposit - gateway creates unique session with payment URL
useCasesPage.casinoStep3: Player completes deposit through privacy-preserving widget using stealth addresses
useCasesPage.casinoStep4: Player requests withdrawal - processed through ZK-private channels for confidentiality
useCasesPage.casinoStep5: Disputes handled through built-in resolution system with automated workflows
useCasesPage.payrollDesc: Companies can pay employees across multiple chains (Solana, Arbitrum, TRON) without revealing individual salaries on-chain. Each payment goes to a unique stealth address, protecting employee privacy while maintaining compliance with labor laws and accounting requirements. Supports batch processing and cross-chain payments for global teams.
useCasesPage.payrollStep1: Company registers meta-addresses for each employee during onboarding
useCasesPage.payrollStep2: HR initiates multi-chain batch payment with individual amounts and preferred chains
useCasesPage.payrollStep3: Each payment creates a unique stealth escrow with recipient-specific keys
useCasesPage.payrollStep4: Employees scan and claim using their view keys from wallet or mobile app
useCasesPage.payrollStep5: No on-chain link between payments and recipients preserves complete privacy
useCasesPage.donationsDesc: Non-profits and charities can accept donations without creating a permanent on-chain link between donors and the organization. Donors get cryptographic proof of their contribution without public exposure, protecting them from unwanted solicitation or political targeting. Supports recurring donations and automatic tax receipt generation.
useCasesPage.donationsStep1: Organization publishes their meta-address on website and donation pages
useCasesPage.donationsStep2: Donor creates stealth payment to the meta-address with chosen amount
useCasesPage.donationsStep3: Organization scans for incoming payments using their view key
useCasesPage.donationsStep4: Donor receives claim receipt and proof for tax deduction purposes
useCasesPage.donationsStep5: No public ledger link between donor and organization preserves anonymity
useCasesPage.ecommerceDesc: Online stores can accept private payments using the multi-chain ZKIRA widget with minimal integration effort. Customers pay with any supported token across Solana, Arbitrum, or TRON, and merchants receive funds without exposing transaction details to competitors. Widget handles wallet connection, chain switching, and transaction signing automatically.
useCasesPage.ecommerceStep1: Install multi-chain widget script tag or React component in checkout flow
useCasesPage.ecommerceStep2: Configure payment amount, accepted tokens, chains, and completion callback
useCasesPage.ecommerceStep3: Customer clicks pay button and widget handles wallet connection and chain selection
useCasesPage.ecommerceStep4: Payment processes through stealth escrow for privacy across chosen chain
useCasesPage.ecommerceStep5: Merchant receives webhook confirmation and can fulfill order
useCasesPage.anonymousTransfersDesc: Use the shielded pool for large anonymous transfers across chains by splitting into fixed-denomination deposits, waiting for soak time, then withdrawing to fresh addresses on different networks. This defeats blockchain forensics by breaking the transaction graph while enabling cross-chain privacy transfers.
useCasesPage.anonymousTransfersStep1: Connect wallet and initialize shielded pool client for source chain
useCasesPage.anonymousTransfersStep2: Split transfer into fixed denominations, each creating an encrypted note
useCasesPage.anonymousTransfersStep3: Wait for 6-hour soak time — protocol generates decoy deposits automatically
useCasesPage.anonymousTransfersStep4: Generate ZK proof and submit private withdrawal to destination chain address
useCasesPage.anonymousTransfersStep5: Funds arrive within ~1 hour via batched processing — no cross-chain link to original deposits
useCasesPage.escrowDesc: Milestone-based escrow for freelance and contract work eliminates payment disputes and builds trust between clients and contractors. Clients lock funds that release incrementally as contractors complete agreed milestones, providing security for both parties with instant settlement and no traditional escrow fees.
useCasesPage.escrowStep1: Client creates milestone escrow with defined deliverables and payment amounts
useCasesPage.escrowStep2: Contractor completes first milestone and submits deliverables
useCasesPage.escrowStep3: Client reviews work and approves milestone release through the platform
useCasesPage.escrowStep4: Funds for that milestone automatically transfer to contractor's stealth address
useCasesPage.escrowStep5: Process repeats for each milestone until project completion
useCasesPage.treasuryDesc: Shared treasury management requiring multiple approvals before any payment executes. Teams, DAOs, and partnerships can set M-of-N approval thresholds while maintaining external privacy through stealth addresses. Supports complex approval workflows, spending limits, and time-locked releases.
useCasesPage.treasuryStep1: Create multi-sig escrow with authorized signers and approval threshold
useCasesPage.treasuryStep2: Any authorized signer proposes a payment with justification
useCasesPage.treasuryStep3: Required number of signers review and approve the proposed payment
useCasesPage.treasuryStep4: Once approval threshold is met, payment executes automatically
useCasesPage.treasuryStep5: All approvals recorded on-chain for internal auditability
useCasesPage.paymentLinksDesc: Generate shareable payment links for invoicing clients, collecting payments from customers, or requesting funds from anyone. Each link creates a unique escrow with expiry dates, custom messages, and support for any SPL token across multiple chains. No account creation required for payers.
useCasesPage.paymentLinksStep1: Create payment link with amount, token, description, and expiry date
useCasesPage.paymentLinksStep2: Share the generated claim URL via email, message, or embed in invoice
useCasesPage.paymentLinksStep3: Payer opens link, connects wallet, and reviews payment details
useCasesPage.paymentLinksStep4: Payment transaction executes and escrow locks funds
useCasesPage.paymentLinksStep5: Recipient claims funds to their stealth address using claim secret

blogPage.title: Blog
blogPage.description: Technical deep-dives, protocol updates, and privacy research
blogPage.casinoGatewayTitle: Launching ZKIRA Casino Gateway: B2B Payment Infrastructure for iGaming
blogPage.casinoGatewayExcerpt: Introducing our comprehensive 36-endpoint gateway API, merchant dashboard, distributor hierarchy, and @zkira/gateway-client SDK for casino and gaming platforms.
blogPage.casinoGatewayProblem1: Online casinos and gaming platforms face unique privacy challenges. Player deposits and withdrawals are fully visible on-chain, exposing betting patterns, win/loss ratios, and financial behavior to competitors and bad actors.
blogPage.casinoGatewayProblem2: Traditional payment processors charge 3-5% fees and impose lengthy settlement delays. Crypto payments offer instant settlement and lower fees, but sacrifice player privacy. ZKIRA Casino Gateway solves both problems.
blogPage.casinoGatewayInfraDesc: ZKIRA Casino Gateway provides everything gaming platforms need to accept private crypto payments:
blogPage.casinoGatewayFeature1Desc: Complete gateway management including sessions, withdrawals, reports, disputes, pools, and distributor hierarchy
blogPage.casinoGatewayFeature2Desc: Web interface for transaction monitoring, balance management, withdrawal approvals, and analytics
blogPage.casinoGatewayFeature3Desc: 4-tier hierarchy (Master → Sub → Agent → Merchant) with automated commission distribution
blogPage.casinoGatewayFeature5Desc: Solana, Arbitrum, and TRON networks with USDC/USDT tokens
blogPage.casinoGatewayHowItWorksDesc: The gateway creates deposit sessions that generate unique payment URLs for each player transaction. Players complete deposits through our privacy-preserving widget, which uses stealth addresses to hide the connection between their wallet and the casino.
blogPage.casinoGatewayQuote: ZKIRA Casino Gateway finally gives us the privacy and infrastructure we need to compete with traditional payment processors while maintaining the speed and cost advantages of crypto. — CTO, Leading Online Casino
blogPage.multiChainTitle: ZKIRA Goes Multi-Chain: Arbitrum and TRON Support
blogPage.multiChainExcerpt: Expanding beyond Solana with native support for Arbitrum and TRON networks, enabling cross-chain private payments while maintaining our signature user experience.
blogPage.multiChainWhy1: The blockchain ecosystem is inherently multi-chain. Users hold assets across Ethereum L2s, alternative L1s, and specialized networks. Forcing them to bridge everything to Solana for privacy creates friction and limits adoption.
blogPage.multiChainWhy2: Different chains serve different use cases: Solana for high-frequency trading and gaming, Arbitrum for DeFi and institutional applications, TRON for stablecoin transfers and emerging markets. By supporting all three, ZKIRA becomes the universal privacy layer.
blogPage.multiChainQuote: Multi-chain privacy isn't just about supporting more networks — it's about making privacy accessible to every crypto user, regardless of their preferred chain. — ZKIRA Engineering Team

docsPage.title: Documentation
docsPage.description: Complete guide to building with ZKIRA Pay
docsPage.gettingStarted_whatIs_paragraph1: ZKIRA Pay is a multi-chain stealth payment protocol that enables truly private transactions using Ed25519 stealth addresses across Solana, Arbitrum, and TRON networks. Unlike traditional blockchain payments where recipient addresses are publicly visible, ZKIRA Pay allows senders to generate one-time stealth addresses that only the intended recipient can identify and claim.
docsPage.gettingStarted_whatIs_paragraph2: The protocol includes a comprehensive casino payment gateway with 36 REST endpoints, merchant dashboard, distributor hierarchy, and B2B infrastructure designed specifically for gaming platforms and enterprise applications requiring financial privacy.
docsPage.gettingStarted_feature1_desc: One-time addresses derived from recipient meta-address using ECDH key exchange
docsPage.gettingStarted_feature2_desc: Native support for Solana, Arbitrum, and TRON networks with unified API
docsPage.gettingStarted_feature3_desc: 36-endpoint B2B API for gaming platforms with deposits, withdrawals, disputes, and pools
docsPage.gettingStarted_feature4_desc: Zero-knowledge proof mixing with fixed denominations and 6-hour soak time
docsPage.gettingStarted_feature5_desc: Web interface for transaction monitoring, withdrawal approvals, and analytics
docsPage.gettingStarted_feature6_desc: 4-tier commission system (Master → Sub → Agent → Merchant) with automated payouts
docsPage.gettingStarted_feature7_desc: @zkira/gateway-client with 26 methods and @zkira/sdk for stealth payments
docsPage.gettingStarted_feature8_desc: Embeddable React hook and HTML script tag for checkout integration
docsPage.gettingStarted_feature9_desc: Optional Tor and Nym mixnet integration for network-level privacy
docsPage.gettingStarted_howItWorks_paragraph1: ZKIRA uses elliptic curve Diffie-Hellman (ECDH) key exchange to derive one-time stealth addresses. When a sender wants to pay a recipient, they use the recipient's published meta-address (a pair of public keys) to generate a unique stealth address that only the recipient can identify and spend from.
docsPage.gettingStarted_howItWorks_paragraph2: For enhanced privacy, the shielded pool uses zero-knowledge proofs to break the on-chain link between deposits and withdrawals. Fixed-denomination deposits with mandatory soak times and decoy transactions make it computationally infeasible to trace funds through the pool.
docsPage.gettingStarted_multiChain_desc: ZKIRA operates natively across Solana, Arbitrum, and TRON networks. Each chain has dedicated smart contracts and infrastructure optimized for its execution environment. The unified API and SDK abstracts chain-specific details, allowing developers to build once and deploy across all supported networks.
docsPage.gettingStarted_prerequisite2: A Solana, Arbitrum, or TRON wallet with supported tokens (USDC/USDT)
docsPage.gettingStarted_prerequisite3: API key from the ZKIRA Developer Dashboard (for gateway integration)
docsPage.gettingStarted_prerequisite4: Basic understanding of blockchain transactions and wallet management
docsPage.gettingStarted_quickSetup_desc: Install the SDK and initialize the client to start building with ZKIRA:
docsPage.stealthAddresses_understanding_paragraph: Stealth addresses are the core privacy primitive in ZKIRA Pay. They allow senders to generate unique one-time addresses for each payment, ensuring that no external observer can link a payment to its intended recipient by analyzing the blockchain.

The system uses Ed25519 elliptic curve cryptography on Solana and secp256k1 on Arbitrum/TRON. Recipients publish a meta-address (a pair of public keys: spend key and view key) which senders use to derive stealth addresses through ECDH shared secret computation.
docsPage.stealthAddresses_metaAddress_paragraph1: A meta-address consists of two Ed25519 public keys: a spend key (used to derive stealth addresses and claim funds) and a view key (used to scan for incoming payments without spending authority).
docsPage.stealthAddresses_metaAddress_paragraph2: Meta-addresses are registered on-chain in the Ghost Registry program, making them discoverable by senders. The registration transaction associates a wallet address with its meta-address, enabling anyone to send private payments to the wallet owner.
docsPage.stealthAddresses_derivation_paragraph1: When sending a payment, the sender generates an ephemeral keypair and computes a shared secret with the recipient's spend key using ECDH. This shared secret is used to derive a new stealth address that only the recipient can identify.
docsPage.stealthAddresses_derivation_paragraph2: The ephemeral public key is published on-chain alongside the payment, allowing the recipient to reconstruct the shared secret and identify the stealth address as theirs.
docsPage.stealthAddresses_derivation_paragraph3: Each payment produces a completely new stealth address, even to the same recipient, making it impossible to link multiple payments to the same person through on-chain analysis.
docsPage.stealthAddresses_scanning_paragraph1: Recipients use their view key to scan the blockchain for incoming payments. The scanning process checks each ephemeral public key against the view key to determine if the corresponding stealth address belongs to the recipient.
docsPage.stealthAddresses_scanning_paragraph2: This scanning can be performed by a third-party service (like the ZKIRA relayer) using only the view key, without any ability to spend the discovered funds. This separation of viewing and spending authority enables secure delegation of payment monitoring.
docsPage.stealthAddresses_claiming_paragraph1: Once a payment is identified through scanning, the recipient uses their spend key to derive the private key corresponding to the stealth address and claim the funds.
docsPage.stealthAddresses_claiming_paragraph2: The claiming process involves creating a transaction that transfers funds from the stealth escrow to the recipient's desired address. This transaction is signed with the derived stealth private key, proving ownership without revealing the recipient's main wallet.
docsPage.paymentFlow_creating_paragraph1: To create a payment, the sender specifies the recipient's meta-address, the amount, and the token. The SDK handles stealth address derivation, escrow creation, and link generation automatically.
docsPage.paymentFlow_creating_paragraph2: Payments are locked in a conditional escrow program on-chain. The escrow holds the funds until claimed by the recipient or refunded by the sender after expiry.
docsPage.paymentFlow_linkGeneration_paragraph1: After creating a payment, a claim URL is generated containing the escrow address and claim secret. This URL can be shared with the recipient through any channel (email, messaging, QR code).
docsPage.paymentFlow_linkGeneration_paragraph2: The claim link contains all information needed for the recipient to claim the payment without needing the sender's cooperation or any additional off-chain coordination.
docsPage.paymentFlow_claiming_paragraph1: Recipients claim payments by visiting the claim URL and connecting their wallet. The SDK verifies the escrow state, derives the stealth private key, and creates the claim transaction.
docsPage.paymentFlow_claiming_paragraph2: The claim process is atomic — funds transfer directly from the escrow to the recipient's wallet in a single transaction. No intermediate steps or additional approvals are required.
docsPage.paymentFlow_refunding_paragraph1: If a payment is not claimed before its expiry time, the sender can refund it. The refund process returns the locked funds from the escrow back to the sender's wallet.
docsPage.paymentFlow_refunding_paragraph2: Refunds are only possible after the expiry time has passed and the payment has not been claimed. This ensures recipients have adequate time to claim their funds.
docsPage.paymentFlow_states_desc: Payments go through the following lifecycle states:
docsPage.casinoGateway_overview_paragraph1: The ZKIRA Casino Gateway is a complete B2B payment infrastructure built for online gaming platforms. It provides 36 REST API endpoints covering deposit sessions, withdrawal processing, dispute resolution, liquidity pool management, and distributor hierarchy administration.
docsPage.casinoGateway_overview_paragraph2: Gaming platforms integrate using the @zkira/gateway-client TypeScript SDK or direct REST API calls. The gateway handles privacy-preserving deposits via stealth addresses, withdrawal processing with configurable approval workflows, and automated commission distribution through the distributor hierarchy.
docsPage.casinoGateway_depositFlow_desc: Deposits are initiated by creating a gateway session. The session generates a unique payment URL that the player uses to complete their deposit through the ZKIRA privacy widget. The widget handles wallet connection, stealth address derivation, and transaction signing.
docsPage.casinoGateway_withdrawalFlow_desc: Withdrawals support two modes: operator-initiated (casino processes withdrawal on behalf of player) and player-initiated (player uses widget to withdraw directly). Both modes support configurable approval workflows and automatic fee deduction.
docsPage.casinoGateway_disputeResolution_desc: The dispute system handles player complaints with structured workflows. Disputes can be created for any transaction, assigned to support agents, and resolved with configurable outcomes including refunds, rejections, or escalations.
docsPage.casinoGateway_poolManagement_desc: Liquidity pools automate balance management for high-volume operations. Pools maintain configurable minimum and maximum balances, automatically rebalancing when thresholds are exceeded. Pool status and utilization metrics are available through the reporting API.
docsPage.merchantDashboard_access_desc: The merchant dashboard is available at /merchant on the ZKIRA app. Authentication uses your merchant API key, which provides access to all dashboard features including transaction monitoring, volume reports, and withdrawal management.
docsPage.merchantDashboard_page1_desc: Volume charts, transaction counts, and key performance metrics at a glance
docsPage.merchantDashboard_page2_desc: Full transaction history with filtering by status, date range, token, and player
docsPage.merchantDashboard_page3_desc: Pending withdrawal queue with approve/reject actions and batch processing
docsPage.merchantDashboard_page4_desc: Active disputes with player details, transaction context, and resolution workflow
docsPage.merchantDashboard_page5_desc: Liquidity pool status, utilization rates, and rebalancing controls
docsPage.merchantDashboard_page6_desc: API key management, webhook configuration, and fee structure settings
docsPage.merchantDashboard_apiKeyAuth_desc: All dashboard API calls use the X-API-Key header for authentication. Keys can be generated, rotated, and revoked through the dashboard settings or programmatically via the API.
docsPage.distributorHierarchy_tierSystem_desc: The ZKIRA distributor hierarchy is a 4-tier system that enables multi-level commission distribution. Each tier has specific roles, commission rates, and permissions within the network.
docsPage.distributorHierarchy_commissionCascade_d: When a merchant processes a transaction, the platform fee is automatically distributed up the distributor chain. Each tier receives their configured commission percentage from the total fee, with ZKIRA retaining the remainder.
docsPage.distributorHierarchy_feeStructure_desc: Distributors can track their downline performance, commission earnings, and transaction volume through the distributor dashboard or the REST API. The API provides detailed breakdowns by time period, merchant, and tier.
docsPage.sdkReference_zkiraClient_desc: The core ZkiraClient provides methods for stealth address operations including payment creation, claiming, scanning, and meta-address management. It works with Solana's web3.js and handles all cryptographic operations internally.
docsPage.sdkReference_gatewayClient_desc: The GatewayClient provides 26 typed methods for interacting with the ZKIRA Casino Gateway API. It handles authentication, request serialization, and response typing for all 36 gateway endpoints.
docsPage.sdkReference_createPaymentLink_desc: Creates a confidential payment escrow with a shareable claim URL. The payment is locked in an on-chain escrow until claimed by the recipient or refunded after expiry.
docsPage.sdkReference_amount_desc: Payment amount in token's smallest unit (e.g., lamports)
docsPage.sdkReference_tokenMint_desc: SPL token mint address (e.g., USDC mint)
docsPage.sdkReference_expirySeconds_desc: Time in seconds before payment can be refunded (optional)
docsPage.widget_reactHook_desc1: The useZkiraPay React hook provides the simplest integration path for React/Next.js applications. It manages wallet connection, payment creation, and status tracking with a single hook call.
docsPage.widget_reactHook_desc2: The hook supports multi-chain configuration, allowing you to specify which networks and tokens to accept. It automatically handles chain switching and wallet adapter selection.
docsPage.widget_htmlIntegration_desc: For non-React applications, the ZKIRA widget can be embedded using a simple script tag. The widget renders a payment form in any HTML container element and handles the entire payment flow.
docsPage.solanaPrograms_overview_desc: ZKIRA deploys three Solana programs (smart contracts) that handle on-chain operations for stealth payments, escrow management, and multi-signature treasury operations.
docsPage.solanaPrograms_ghostRegistry_desc: Stores meta-address registrations linking wallet addresses to their stealth public keys. Enables senders to discover recipient meta-addresses for stealth payment derivation.
docsPage.solanaPrograms_conditionalEscrow_desc: Holds payment funds in escrow with time-based expiry conditions. Supports claim by stealth key holder and refund by sender after expiry. Processes under 200k compute units per instruction.
docsPage.solanaPrograms_multisigEscrow_desc: Enables M-of-N approval workflows for treasury operations. Multiple authorized signers must approve before funds are released, providing governance controls for team and DAO treasuries.
docsPage.shieldedPool_overview_desc1: The shielded pool is a zero-knowledge proof mixing system that breaks the on-chain link between deposits and withdrawals. Users deposit fixed denominations of tokens and later withdraw to fresh addresses, with ZK proofs ensuring no external observer can connect deposits to withdrawals.
docsPage.shieldedPool_overview_desc2: The pool operates on both Solana and Arbitrum with chain-specific implementations optimized for each execution environment. Fixed denominations (10 USDC on Solana, 0.1/1/10/100 ETH on Arbitrum) ensure uniform deposit amounts that prevent amount-based correlation attacks.
docsPage.shieldedPool_deposits_desc: Depositing into the shielded pool generates a cryptographic commitment and an encrypted note. The commitment is stored on-chain in a Merkle tree, while the note is kept secret by the depositor and used later for withdrawal.
docsPage.shieldedPool_withdrawals_desc: Withdrawals generate a zero-knowledge proof demonstrating knowledge of a valid deposit note without revealing which deposit it corresponds to. Withdrawals are batched and processed by the relayer to prevent timing correlation.
docsPage.shieldedPool_zkProofs_desc: The shielded pool uses Groth16 ZK-SNARKs for withdrawal proofs. Each proof demonstrates four properties:
docsPage.shieldedPool_proof1_desc: The note corresponds to a valid commitment in the Merkle tree
docsPage.shieldedPool_proof2_desc: The nullifier has not been used before (prevents spending the same note twice)
docsPage.shieldedPool_proof3_desc: The prover knows the secret note (proving deposit ownership)
docsPage.shieldedPool_proof4_desc: The withdrawal amount matches the deposit denomination exactly
docsPage.shieldedPool_privacyGuarantees_desc: The shielded pool provides strong privacy guarantees against various attack vectors:
docsPage.shieldedPool_mechanism3: Protocol generates fake deposits to obscure real activity
docsPage.shieldedPool_mechanism4: No on-chain link between deposit and withdrawal
docsPage.shieldedPool_mechanism6: Rate limiting on deposits per address
docsPage.shieldedPool_mechanism7: Withdrawals processed in batches by relayer
docsPage.shieldedPool_mechanism8: Optional Tor/Nym integration for IP privacy
docsPage.shieldedPool_resistanceLevels_desc: The shielded pool provides multiple levels of privacy resistance:
docsPage.shieldedPool_resistance1_desc: Transaction graph broken — no direct on-chain link between deposit and withdrawal addresses
docsPage.shieldedPool_resistance2_desc: Timing resistance — mandatory soak time with decoy transactions prevents temporal correlation
docsPage.shieldedPool_resistance3_desc: Amount resistance — fixed denominations prevent value-based correlation across deposits
docsPage.shieldedPool_resistance4_desc: Network-level privacy via Tor/Nym mixnet integration prevents IP address correlation
docsPage.shieldedPool_limitation1: Fixed denominations only — custom amounts require splitting into multiple deposits
docsPage.shieldedPool_limitation2: 6-hour minimum soak time — not suitable for time-sensitive transfers
docsPage.shieldedPool_limitation3: ZK proof generation takes ~40 seconds on consumer hardware
docsPage.shieldedPool_limitation4: Batched withdrawals processed within ~1 hour (not instant)
docsPage.shieldedPool_limitation5: Cross-chain withdrawals require separate pool instances per network
